pattern: Any element that divides every member of a set must divide their greatest common divisor, so the task reduces to finding the smallest array value that divides one computed gcd.
hints: An element divides all of numsDivide exactly when it divides the gcd of numsDivide, so compute that gcd once.
You delete elements from the bottom up, so the surviving smallest element is the smallest value of nums that divides that gcd.
Compute g = gcd(numsDivide). Sort nums; the answer is the count of leading elements that do not divide g, or -1 if none divides g.

edge_inputs.description: smallest does not divide but a duplicate larger value does
delete several equal smallest values before a divisor remains

stress.description: random arrays with a planted divisor so the answer is finite
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    g = rng.randint(2, 1000)
    numsDivide = [g * rng.randint(1, 1000) for _ in range(rng.randint(1, n))]
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10**6) for _ in range(n)]
    # ensure at least one divisor of gcd exists
    nums.append(g)
    return [nums, numsDivide]
stress.note: guarantees a feasible answer plus many non-dividing values to skip

complexity.time: O(n log n + m log V)
